steps.label: Local police station (SHO)
steps.description_template: Register a written complaint or FIR for cognizable offences. Confirm which station has territorial jurisdiction for {district} on the official state police portal.

steps.label: Superintendent of Police (SP)
steps.description_template: Escalate if the station refuses to register a cognizable FIR without lawful grounds, or if investigation is unreasonably delayed. Follow official circulars and written representations only.

steps.label: Judicial remedies (where legally advised)
steps.description_template: Where facts support it, consult a qualified advocate about judicial routes (for example magistrate complaints) using India Code and official court information — not social media summaries.

steps.label: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal
steps.description_template: Report cyber fraud and online scams through the official Ministry of Home Affairs cybercrime portal. Preserve screenshots, transaction IDs, and bank timelines before filing.

steps.label: Local cyber police / economic offences wing
steps.description_template: Follow up with the cyber cell or police station handling {district} matters as directed on the portal or by the helpline. Carry printouts of your online report.

steps.label: Bank / wallet dispute window
steps.description_template: Immediately use official banking channels (branch, toll-free, or in-app dispute) for unauthorised debits; keep reference numbers for the police complaint.

steps.label: Labour authority (conciliation / inspection)
steps.description_template: Approach the district labour office or prescribed authority for unpaid wages, PF, gratuity, or wrongful termination under applicable labour laws. Use only official labour department portals for {district}.

steps.label: Written representation to employer
steps.description_template: Send a dated written representation with pay slips, appointment letter, and bank statements via registered post or official email as per your employment records.

steps.label: Tribunal or civil forum (where applicable)
steps.description_template: If conciliation fails or the dispute falls outside a labour officer’s scope, confirm the correct tribunal or civil court from official government legal services or a qualified advocate.

steps.label: Seller / service provider grievance desk
steps.description_template: Raise a written complaint with the brand’s official grievance officer contact published on invoices or websites. Keep delivery proofs and warranty documents.

steps.label: District Consumer Commission (e-daakhil)
steps.description_template: File or prepare a consumer complaint through the official e-daakhil portal for {district}, with bills and correspondence. Verify filing fees and pecuniary limits on the commission’s official site.

steps.label: State consumer commission (appeal route)
steps.description_template: If an order is passed against you or you need an appeal, confirm timelines and fees only from official consumer commission notifications or India Code.

steps.label: Pre-litigation notice (where appropriate)
steps.description_template: Many civil disputes start with a legal notice through a qualified advocate. Do not rely on template tone alone — facts and limitation periods matter.

steps.label: Correct civil court / revenue forum
steps.description_template: Identify pecuniary jurisdiction and territorial jurisdiction for {district} using official court websites or district administration portals — not generic blog posts.

steps.label: Mediation / Lok Adalat (optional)
steps.description_template: Explore court-annexed mediation or legal services authority programmes through official court or DLSA channels when suitable.

steps.label: Municipal corporation / nagar palika
steps.description_template: Report civic issues (roads, drains, sanitation) through the official municipal portal or ward office for {district}. Obtain a complaint reference number.

steps.label: District magistrate / collector grievance (where applicable)
steps.description_template: Some civic failures may be escalated through official district administration grievance mechanisms — confirm the current channel on the district’s .gov.in site.

steps.label: Public representatives (informational)
steps.description_template: Local elected representatives may forward complaints to departments; this does not replace the statutory municipal or revenue route.

steps.label: Identify the public information officer (PIO)
steps.description_template: Locate the correct PIO and fee rules on the official RTI portal or the department’s .gov.in site for the office that holds the information.

steps.label: File RTI in prescribed format
steps.description_template: Submit a concise application with fee proof; avoid vague questions. For {district}, use the department’s notified submission mode (online or counter).

steps.label: First appeal / complaint (if needed)
steps.description_template: If the reply is missing or unsatisfactory, follow the official first appeal route to the appellate authority within the statutory period.

steps.label: Confirm the correct department
steps.description_template: Use official .gov.in portals for {district} to identify whether the issue is police, labour, consumer, revenue, or municipal — avoid guessing from social media.

steps.label: Collect documents and timelines
steps.description_template: Prepare dated evidence (messages, contracts, bills) and note limitation periods from primary legal sources or a qualified advocate.
